title: Trace CSS from Source to Computed Output
summary: Trace CSS from Source to Computed Output retrieves prior skills in an original community survey stylesheet parsing and rendering trace scenario and requires implementation, behavior, failure-state, and transfer evidence.
objectives: Apply css rules syntax in unfamiliar constraints.
Produce or audit a real community survey stylesheet parsing and rendering trace artifact without happy-path-only reasoning.
Preserve earlier requirements while testing keyboard, narrow-width, invalid, and assistive-semantic states.

starterFiles.html: <!-- Continue the Community Needs Survey artifact and connect styles.css. -->

steps.title: 04 · Read the community survey stylesheet parsing and rendering trace contract · theory
steps.instruction: Checkpoint 4: Which practice survives changed data and devices? Scenario: community survey stylesheet parsing and rendering trace.
steps.why: Trace CSS from Source to Computed Output makes the decision observable and testable. This is checkpoint 4 in community survey stylesheet parsing and rendering trace.

content.text: A CSS rule combines a selector with a declaration block. Each declaration has a property and value. Comments document intent, and at-rules add conditional or structural instructions. Browsers normally discard an invalid declaration or rule and continue parsing later valid input, so DevTools source, parsed rules, warnings, and computed values reveal the actual failure boundary. Apply it to community survey stylesheet parsing and rendering trace.
